Portal via net4sats.lan

Attempted to load portal via http://net4sats.lan:2050/
FAIL
🔍 Z.AI Vision Analysis: DNS_PROBE_STARTED error — 'net4sats.lan's DNS address could not be found.' Chrome on Android uses its own DNS resolver (DNS over HTTPS) which bypasses the router's dnsmasq. The .lan domain only resolves via the router's local DNS.
  • ⚠️ Chrome secure DNS bypasses router dnsmasq — .lan domains not resolvable
  • ⚠️ Portal only accessible via IP address, not domain name

🔍 Key Findings & Recommendations

✅ Portal UI Quality: Excellent (9/10)

The portal page renders correctly when accessed via IP address (192.168.1.1:2050). Full net4sats branding, clean layout, clear pricing (5 sats → 100 MB), Lightning/Cashu tabs, Generate Invoice button. Z.AI vision rated the UX 9/10.

❌ Critical: Chrome Secure DNS Bypasses Router DNS

Chrome on Android uses DNS over HTTPS (DoH) by default, which bypasses the router's dnsmasq. This means .lan domains (net4sats.lan, tollgate.lan) cannot be resolved. Only IP-based access works.

Impact: Portal accessible via IP only, not domain name. Admin panel inaccessible via tollgate.lan in Chrome.
Fix options:
1. Accept IP-based access (captive portal popup uses IP anyway)
2. Use a real domain (net4sats.cash) for DNS resolution
3. Disable Chrome DoH on test device (not practical for production)
4. Configure dnsmasq to intercept DNS-over-HTTPS (complex)

❌ CaptivePortalLogin Timing Issue

The CaptivePortalLogin activity showed a Google error page instead of the portal. This is likely a timing issue — Android opens the activity before NDS has fully intercepted the traffic. The portal redirect hadn't triggered when the screenshot was taken.

Fix: Add a delay (5-10s) after opening CaptivePortalLogin before capturing screenshot. Or verify the portal loads by checking UI elements.

✅ Payment API Works Reliably

Lightning payment via API (POST /ln-invoice) works consistently — invoice created instantly, FakeWallet pays within 3 seconds. The payment infrastructure is solid.

⚠️ Internet Verification Needs Delay

After payment success, internet access doesn't work immediately. NDS needs a moment to propagate the authentication. Adding a 5-second delay after payment before testing internet would resolve false failures.
